sourceMovie.title: Revolver
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Revolver for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Guy Ritchie keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lucky Number Slevin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Revolver clicked for you, Lucky Number Slevin (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Last Days of American Crime

faq.question: What should I watch after Revolver?
faq.answer: Try Lucky Number Slevin, The Last Days of American Crime, Accident Man—each shares DNA with Revolver while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Revolver?
faq.answer: Revolver (2005) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Revolver worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
